footer.tagline: Static-site localization for public growth pages.
home.hero.title: Launch SEO-ready localized versions of your website without rebuilding it.
home.hero.description: Preview public pages in another language before production rollout.
home.features.ai.title: AI speed. Human control.
home.features.ai.description: Use glossary and override controls where enabled so public-page copy keeps approved terms and placeholders intact.
home.features.sync.title: Source snapshots you choose
home.features.sync.description: Refresh localized pages through crawl and translate workflows instead of promising live source mirroring.
home.features.seo.title: Native SEO & performance
home.features.seo.description: Generate localized metadata, hreflang tags, and optimized pages for rollout review.
home.features.subtitle: A focused workflow for crawlable localized public website pages.

home.cta.description: Generate a private preview of one public page and see how WebLingo handles localized copy, SEO metadata, and page structure before you publish.
pricing.header.title: Scope WebLingo after a private preview
pricing.header.description: Public pricing and self-serve checkout are not launch promises yet. Generate a private preview first, then talk through a pilot for public pages.

contact.header.title: Talk through a public-page rollout
contact.header.description: Tell us about the public pages, markets, and constraints you want to evaluate. Contact submissions are simple recent messages, not a durable sales workflow.

contact.direct.description: Reach us at contact@weblingo.app for private-preview or production-pilot questions.
try.header.title: Preview WebLingo on your own site
try.header.description: Enter a public URL and an email. We'll generate a private preview and email the link when it's ready.
try.form.emailHint: Generating the demo takes a few minutes — we email you the links when it's ready.
try.form.emailRequired: Enter your email. The demo takes a few minutes and we email you the link when it's ready.
try.form.invalidUrl: Enter a valid URL that starts with http or https.
try.form.sameLanguage: Source and target languages must be different.
try.preview.emailedNotice: We also emailed these links to {email}.
try.status.emailNotice: Usually ready in a few minutes. We'll email the links to {email}, so it's safe to close this tab.
try.status.processingHint: This can take a few minutes; you can close this tab.
try.status.capacityHint: Rendering capacity is temporarily full. We'll keep retrying automatically.
try.status.providerCapacityHint: Translation capacity is temporarily full. We'll keep retrying automatically.
try.status.statusUnreachable: We can't check the preview status right now. We'll keep retrying.
try.status.ready: Your demo is ready. We'll also email these links so you can return later.
try.progress.label: Preview progress
try.error.invalid_url: We couldn't preview this URL. Please check it and try again.
try.error.blocked_host: This site blocks automated previews. Try another URL.
try.error.dns_failed: We couldn't reach this domain. Please check the URL and try again.
try.error.dns_timeout: The domain lookup timed out. Please try again.
try.error.page_too_large: This page is too large to preview.
try.error.render_failed: We couldn't render the page for preview.
try.error.template_decode_failed: We couldn't decode the preview HTML.
try.error.waf_blocked: Preview was blocked by the site firewall.
try.error.processing_timeout: Preview processing timed out. Please try again.
try.error.queue_enqueue_failed: Preview service is busy. Please try again in a moment.
try.error.provision_failed: We couldn't set up the demo environment for this page. Please try again in a few minutes.
try.error.provision_stalled: Setting up the demo took longer than expected, so we stopped this run. Please try again.
try.error.processing_stalled: This preview took longer than expected, so we stopped this run. Please try again.
try.error.translation_capacity_exhausted: Our translation provider is at capacity right now, so this run could not finish. Please try again in a little while.
try.error.source_page_too_large: This page is too large for a demo preview. Try a smaller page from the same site, such as the homepage.
try.error.showcase_failed: We couldn't build the demo showcase for this page. Please try again in a few minutes.
try.error.translation_failed: We couldn't translate this page. Check that the URL is public and correct, then try again.
try.error.referenceHint: If you contact support, include reference {ref}.
try.status.pendingEmail: Processing is taking longer than expected. We'll email {email} when your preview is ready.
try.center.capacityHint: Rendering capacity is temporarily full. We'll keep retrying automatically.
try.center.providerCapacityHint: Waiting for translation capacity. We'll retry automatically.
docs.title: Getting started
docs.overview: Evaluate public-page localization in four steps.
docs.step.one: Generate a private preview for one public URL.
docs.step.two: Review localized copy, metadata, and page structure.
docs.step.three: Scope production routes, domains, exclusions, and source snapshots.
docs.step.four: Enable serving only after rollout terms and controls are confirmed.

dashboard.site.settings.crawlCapture.des: Choose how we capture HTML for segmentation and runtime dictionary coverage.
dashboard.site.settings.crawlCapture.hel: Template+hydrated is best for most sites. Use hydrated-only if JS is required to render meaningful content.
dashboard.site.settings.clientRuntime.de: Inject a runtime dictionary so client-side renders stay translated.
dashboard.site.settings.clientRuntime.he: Recommended for SPA/hydrated sites to prevent source-language re-renders.
dashboard.site.settings.spaRefresh.title: Client-side navigation support
dashboard.site.settings.spaRefresh.descr: Enable this if your site changes pages without a full reload. WebLingo will fetch route-scoped dictionaries on navigation for better coverage.
dashboard.site.settings.spaRefresh.label: Enable client-side navigation support
dashboard.site.settings.spaRefresh.help: Turn this on for in-page route changes (URL changes but the document does not reload).
dashboard.site.settings.spaRefresh.note: Uses /.well-known/weblingo/dict/... on navigation. If CSP blocks connect-src, refresh will not run.
dashboard.site.settings.spaRefresh.missi: When a route dictionary is missing, choose Global-only (safer) or Baseline (more coverage with higher collision risk).
dashboard.site.settings.spaRefresh.error: When the dictionary fetch fails (network/CSP), choose Global-only (safer) or Baseline (more coverage).
dashboard.site.settings.spaRefresh.secti: Uses section dictionaries when route dicts are missing. Increases the candidate pool; enable only when needed.
dashboard.site.settings.translatableAttr: Translate select HTML attributes (tooltips, labels) without editing the source site.
Comma-separated attribute names. Only data-* and aria-* attributes are allowed.
dashboard.site.settings.lockedHelp: This setting is locked for your account. Contact support to enable editing.
dashboard.site.settings.demoLockedHelp: This setting is shown from the real dashboard but stays read-only until activation.
dashboard.site.settings.demoLockedDescri: This is the real settings page for the claimed site, but edits are not saved until activation.
dashboard.site.settings.demoSummaryDescr: Use this page to inspect routing, brand voice, language routing, and integration settings. Activation is required before any setting is saved.

landing.expansion.pain.items.2.title: Unsupported pages create risk

landing.expansion.pain.items.3.title: Manual page copies drift
landing.expansion.pain.items.3.body: Duplicated CMS pages and ad hoc translations drift as pages change.

landing.expansion.cost.body: Language preference can shape buying research, but WebLingo does not guarantee ranking, traffic, or conversion lift.

landing.trust.items.2: Edge/CDN delivery for published deployment artifacts.
landing.trust.items.3: Public-only by default with robots allowlist control.
landing.faq.title: Questions engineers ask
landing.faq.items.1.a: WebLingo renders crawlable localized HTML with metadata and hreflang support. It does not guarantee rankings or traffic.
landing.faq.items.2.q: Do you access private or authenticated pages?
landing.faq.items.2.a: No. WebLingo processes public URLs you provide. Use allowlists for paths like /blog or /docs; logged-in, personalized, and real-time pages are out of scope.
landing.faq.items.3.a: Refreshes run through crawl and translate workflows from approved source snapshots, not live mirroring.
landing.faq.items.4.q: Can I control which parts of my site are localized?
landing.faq.items.4.a: Yes. You can restrict localization to specific paths and exclude others, focusing on the content that matters most.
landing.faq.items.5.q: Does this work with React, Next.js, or SPAs?
landing.faq.items.5.a: WebLingo works when pages render at crawl time, including SSR/SSG and hydrated JS. Logged-in, personalized, and real-time pages are out of scope.
landing.faq.items.6.q: Do you translate URL paths or slugs?
landing.faq.items.6.a: Yes. Keep original paths or choose translated slugs per language, with hreflang and localized sitemap support.

legal.common.canonicalNotice: If there is any discrepancy between translations, the English version prevails.
legal.terms.title: Terms of Service
legal.terms.sections.operator.title: 1. Operator
legal.terms.sections.operator.body: WebLingo is a service operated by Bredis SAS.
legal.terms.sections.operator.list:
Bredis SAS — Société par Actions Simplifiée (SAS)
SIREN 822 881 553 — VAT FR06822881553
44 Rue Victor Hugo, 29200 Brest, France
contact@weblingo.app
legal.terms.sections.service.title: 2. Description of service
legal.terms.sections.service.body: WebLingo provides private previews and, where separately agreed, public-page localization serving for static-heavy website pages. Production scope and billing terms are confirmed separately.
legal.terms.sections.subscriptions.title: 3. Subscriptions & payments
legal.terms.sections.subscriptions.body: Where a paid pilot or subscription is separately agreed, the applicable order or agreement defines the billing rules:
legal.terms.sections.subscriptions.list:
Payment processor, renewal, cancellation, and refund terms are confirmed in the applicable order or pilot agreement.
Public launch pages do not create self-serve billing or production entitlements.
Preview requests do not require DNS publishing.
legal.terms.sections.responsibilities.bo: You remain responsible for how WebLingo is used within your organization:
legal.terms.sections.responsibilities.li:
Verify translation accuracy before publishing localized pages.
Respect third-party rights, including copyright and trademarks.
Do not upload or generate unlawful, infringing, or harmful content.
legal.terms.sections.dataHosting.title: 5. Data & hosting
legal.terms.sections.dataHosting.body: WebLingo stores translated pages and translation dictionaries on secure infrastructure operated by Vercel (USA) and Cloudflare (global). Payment data is never stored by WebLingo and is processed directly by Stripe.
legal.terms.sections.intellectual.title: 6. Intellectual property
legal.terms.sections.intellectual.body: All WebLingo source code, designs, and trademarks are owned by Bredis SAS. You retain ownership of your original website content and the translations generated through the service.
legal.terms.sections.liability.title: 7. Limitation of liability
legal.terms.sections.liability.body: WebLingo is provided on an "as-is" basis without warranty. Bredis SAS is not liable for translation errors, downtime, loss of data, or any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.
legal.terms.sections.governingLaw.title: 8. Governing law
legal.terms.sections.governingLaw.body: These Terms are governed by French law. Any dispute shall be settled before the courts of Brest, France.
legal.privacy.title: Privacy Policy

contact@weblingo.app
legal.privacy.sections.collect.title: 2. Data we collect
legal.privacy.sections.collect.body: We only collect the information required to operate, secure, improve, and bill the service:
legal.privacy.sections.collect.list:
Email address for account access and billing notifications.
Website URL to perform translation and localization.
Payment data processed by Stripe (never stored by WebLingo).
Product analytics identifiers and metadata, including internal user and account IDs, plan/status, workspace audience, event names, device/session metadata, and privacy-protected replay events.
legal.privacy.sections.analytics.title: 3. Analytics
legal.privacy.sections.analytics.body:
WebLingo uses PostHog EU Cloud for product analytics, error and reliability signals, and privacy-protected session replay on allowlisted public or sanitized website pages. Replay is disabled on the logged-in dashboard, preview-content pages, translation editing, runtime observation, glossary and override controls, admin routes, and customer-content pages. For the deployed public service, browser analytics requests route through WebLingo's PostHog managed proxy at metrics.weblingo.app; while this managed proxy beta is enabled, PostHog routes that proxy traffic through Cloudflare. PostHog receives pseudonymous event data and dashboard identifiers such as internal user/account IDs, plan/status, and workspace audience; it does not receive payment card details.

Browser capture is configured without autocapture, automatic pageview/pageleave capture, cookies or persistent browser storage, heatmaps, console logs, performance capture, or network request payloads. Session replay is sampled, masks all inputs and page text, disables canvas recording, and drops captured network requests.
legal.privacy.sections.storage.title: 4. Data storage
legal.privacy.sections.storage.body: Localized pages and translation dictionaries are stored on infrastructure operated by Vercel (USA) and Cloudflare (global). Product analytics and replay data are processed in PostHog EU Cloud.
legal.privacy.sections.rights.title: 5. Your rights (GDPR)
legal.privacy.sections.rights.body: You may request access to, correction of, deletion of, or objection to processing of your personal data at any time by emailing contact@weblingo.app.
legal.privacy.sections.legalBasis.title: 6. Legal basis
legal.privacy.sections.legalBasis.body: Processing is based on contract performance for registered users and on WebLingo's legitimate interest in pseudonymous, privacy-limited product analytics and service reliability.
legal.privacy.sections.retention.title: 7. Data retention
legal.privacy.sections.retention.body: We retain account data while your account remains active or as required by applicable law. PostHog session replays are retained for 30 days.
legal.privacy.sections.updates.title: 8. Updates
legal.privacy.sections.updates.body: We may update this Privacy Policy at any time. The latest version is always available at https://www.weblingo.app/legal.

pricing.faq.title: FAQ
pricing.faq.description: Common questions before you generate a private preview or scope a production pilot.
pricing.faq.items.languages.question: Can we evaluate more than one target language?
pricing.faq.items.languages.answer: Start with one private preview. Additional languages and page groups are scoped during pilot planning.
pricing.faq.items.traffic.question: Do you publish traffic or bandwidth promises?
pricing.faq.items.traffic.answer: No. Commercial limits and production terms are confirmed only after billing, entitlement, and rollout requirements are reviewed.
pricing.faq.items.setup.question: How does setup work?
pricing.faq.items.setup.answer: Preview requires only a submitted public URL. Production rollout usually involves domain or route configuration, source selection, review, and serving controls.
pricing.faq.items.cancel.question: Are self-serve billing changes available?
pricing.faq.items.cancel.answer: Do not treat public launch copy as a billing portal or runtime entitlement promise. Billing behavior is confirmed during pilot scoping.
pricing.faq.items.agencies.question: Can agencies manage client billing here?
pricing.faq.items.agencies.answer: Agency rebilling, shared workspaces, team management, and white-label production are not launch promises. Agency design-partner pilots are scoped manually.
pricing.faq.items.scope.question: What pages are in scope?
pricing.faq.items.scope.answer: Public landing pages, docs, pricing, policies, tourism pages, and SEO pages are in scope. Checkout, booking, account, app UI, personalized, and real-time flows are excluded.
pricing.faq.items.checkout.question: Can checkout activate production automatically?
pricing.faq.items.checkout.answer: No. Public checkout activation is not promised until billing and backend runtime entitlement authority are connected and tested.
pricing.faq.items.agency.question: Is there an agency package?
pricing.faq.items.agency.answer: Agency design-partner previews can be scoped manually, but centralized billing, client workspaces, rebilling, team management, and support SLAs are not launch promises.
pricing.faq.items.updates.question: Will localized pages stay live-updated?
pricing.faq.items.updates.answer: Refreshes run through crawl and translate workflows from approved source snapshots. Avoid treating this as always-on source mirroring or auto-publish.
pricing.faq.contact: Need rollout help? Talk through a public-page pilot.
pricing.header.fact: One account and subscription covers one website; agencies or additional websites are scoped separately.

home.hero.trust: No code installed for preview. Publish only after review.
home.problem.title: Public-page localization without pretending every workflow is static.
home.problem.description: Marketing, docs, pricing, and policy pages can be localized as crawlable HTML. Checkout, booking, account, app UI, personalized, and real-time flows stay out of scope.
home.problem.solution: WebLingo crawls public site snapshots, extracts translatable content, applies controlled translation rules, renders deterministic localized HTML, and serves published deployment artifacts from edge storage.
home.steps.1.title: Step 1 — Choose public pages
home.steps.1.description: Start with a public URL and exclude checkout, logged-in, personalized, booking, and real-time flows.
home.steps.2.title: Step 2 — Generate a private preview
home.steps.2.description: Inspect localized visible copy, metadata, page structure, and supported SEO output before production.
home.steps.3.title: Step 3 — Scope production deliberately
home.steps.3.description: Use approved source snapshots, serving controls, and rollout planning instead of auto-publish promises.
home.benefits.1: Crawlable localized pages — published deployment artifacts can be served from edge storage.
home.benefits.2: Control tools — refine public-page translations with glossary and overrides where enabled.
home.benefits.3: SEO-ready output — translated slugs, hreflang, metadata, and canonical behavior are part of rollout review.
home.benefits.4: Snapshot refreshes — update through crawl and translate workflows instead of live source mirroring.
home.benefits.5: Translate from any source language and choose 35+ target locales (Japanese, English, French, Chinese, Korean, and more). Need another? Contact us — we’re expanding support continuously.
home.languages.title: Translate from your language to the ones your customers speak
home.languages.body: WebLingo detects your source content automatically and lets you select the target locales that matter for your rollout.
home.languages.supportedIntro: We currently support 35+ languages for marketing, help center, and documentation sites.
home.languages.supportedExamples: Popular choices include Japanese, English, French, Chinese (Simplified & Traditional), Korean, Portuguese, Spanish, German, and many more.
home.languages.link: View the full list of supported languages.
home.languages.future: Need another language? Let’s talk — we’re adding more soon.

home.trust.placeholder: Trusted by early adopters across Japan and beyond.
home.faq.q1.question: Do I need to update my website code?
home.faq.q1.answer: No. You don’t need to change your website, but you can optionally add a link to your translated pages. Contact us if you need help adding it.
home.faq.q2.question: Can ecommerce, booking, or app workflows be translated?
home.faq.q2.answer: No. WebLingo launch scope is public pages such as marketing, docs, pricing, policies, tourism pages, and SEO pages.
home.faq.q3.question: Are there usage limits?
home.faq.q3.answer: Public launch copy does not publish traffic, bandwidth, page, or billing guarantees. Production limits are scoped during pilot planning.
home.faq.q4.question: Is hosting included?
home.faq.q4.answer: Published deployment artifacts can be served from edge storage after production setup, but preview does not imply production DNS is complete.
home.faq.q5.question: Can I change plans later?
home.faq.q5.answer: Do not treat launch copy as a self-serve billing promise. Production and billing terms are confirmed during rollout scoping.
